items.acceptance_criteria: Document listing all major modules and their purposes

items.description: Conduct literature review via web search
items.acceptance_criteria: At least 10 relevant papers cited in sources.bib, identifying state-of-the-art computational bounds for the Chvátal-Sankoff constant.
sources.bib file exists in repo root with BibTeX entries for all consulted sources, ensuring baseline works (CS1975, H2024, L2009, DP1995) are included.

items.description: Subagent delegation: Deep dive into bounds techniques
items.acceptance_criteria: Subagent report summarizing deterministic vs probabilistic bounding strategies for Chvátal-Sankoff from the literature.
Folder results/concept_evolve/tree/ created with initial problem definition nodes formatted as markdown or JSON files.

items.description: Implement baseline Exact LCS string generator
items.acceptance_criteria: Script capable of exactly computing expected LCS for strings up to n=15.
Script computing upper bound that reproduces the 0.826280 result for baseline configuration.
Script computing lower bound that reproduces the 0.792665992 result for baseline configuration.
Subagent execution logs systematically verifying implemented baseline accuracy vs known results from the literature review.
Test suite implemented for continuous evaluation of bound tightness and computation time across configurations.
At least 5 novel lower-bound state machine concepts documented systematically in results/concept_evolve/tree/.
At least 5 novel upper-bound alignment models documented systematically in results/concept_evolve/tree/.

items.description: Develop extended finite automata for lower bound
items.acceptance_criteria: Code implementation of a new finite automata scaling state tracking beyond H2024's capacity.

items.description: Develop improved weighting scheme for upper bound
items.acceptance_criteria: Code implementation of an alignment graph with a tighter density distribution function than L2009.

items.description: Subagent delegation: Rapid prototyping of novel concepts
items.acceptance_criteria: Subagent synthesis report comparing the empirical promise of the 10 concepts generated in items 011 and 012.
Computational results yielding a provable lower bound > 0.792665992.
Computational results yielding a provable upper bound < 0.826280.
Subagent automated experiment logs demonstrating optimized state transition bounds and matrix relaxations.
Quantitative report section detailing the absolute margin of improvement over the bounds established in the Phase 1 literature review.
Document analyzing the asymptotic behavior and scaling limits of the new bounding strategies.
Draft document (LaTeX/Markdown) generated by a subagent summarizing the methodology, experiments, and citations from sources.bib.
Complete README.md and final codebase integrating the new bounds, metrics, and linking clearly to sources.bib.
